line: - Updated stuck action execution and logging in `crates/saw-daemon/src/alerter.rs`
- The daemon alerter already had the enum/action plumbing, but actions were phase-rate-limited rather than action-rate-limited and warn/bell did not emit operator-facing stderr output.
- `checkpoint-kill` was partially wired already; the missing user-visible behavior was explicit stderr alerting plus end-to-end validation.
- Using `nix::sys::signal::kill` simplified SIGINT/SIGKILL delivery and matched the task requirement better than the previous mixed sysinfo/shell fallback.
- Rate limiting is now keyed by action name (`warn`, `bell`, `kill`, `checkpoint-kill`) for 60 seconds, matching the requirement to avoid repeating the same action.
- Alert actions now always write a clear stderr message; `bell` additionally emits `\x07`.
- Human alert lines are no longer duplicated on stdout in watch mode; stderr is the source of operator-facing action output, while robot mode still emits JSON alerts on stdout.
- Kept existing dead-phase suggestion text: `restart the Claude process to resume monitoring`.
- Full test suite passes, but there is a pre-existing warning for unused function `find_session_jsonl` in `src/cmd/common.rs:303`.
- The API hang e2e script emits benign shell `wait_for: No record of process ...` lines during cleanup under this environment, but the test passes.
